name: Shelton Brooks
description: Canadian-born African American composer of popular music and jazz
abstract: Shelton Brooks (May 4, 1886 – September 6, 1975) was a Canadian-born American composer and performer of popular music and jazz. He was known for his ragtime and vaudeville style, and wrote some of the biggest hits of the first third of the 20th century; including "Some of These Days" and "At the Darktown Strutters' Ball".

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: Then I played at Streets of Paris and then we played at the Swanee Inn with Shelton Brooks

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, that was Mr. Shelton Brooks... I think he liked me because I sang his song... So Mr. Shelton Brooks said, 'That was good...'

about_quotes.speaker: Glover Compton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Isn't that where Shelton Brooks wrote? ... [INTERVIEWEE] All the great stars of the stage used to come there.

about_quotes.speaker: Alphonse Picou
about_quotes.evidence: I think I was by Shelton Brooks. Yeah, Walking the Dog, yeah. He's the one who wrote Darktown Strutters Ball.
